specification.topic-statement: The root agent initialization ensures that exactly one root agent record exists in the database, creating it on first run and adopting any orphaned messages from pre-agent-table schema versions.

scope.in-scope: Root agent existence check, new agent record creation, orphan message adoption, and initial fork event insertion for pinned document replay.
scope.boundary: This initialization is called once during application startup as part of the database setup sequence. Startup ordering is covered by the session startup specification.
Schema version detection (checking for the agent_uuid column) relies on the migration system. Schema management is covered by the database migration specification.

behavior.existence-check: Queries for any agent record where parent_uuid is null (root agents). If at least one row is returned, the first result's UUID is returned as the existing root agent UUID and no further steps are performed. The operation is idempotent.
behavior.new-agent-creation:
If no root agent exists, a new UUID is generated and a new agent record is inserted with: - parent_uuid: null (marks it as a root agent) - status: 'running' - name: null - created_at: current time - fork_message_id: 0 - session_id: the current session identifier
The new UUID is returned to the caller.
behavior.orphan-adoption:
Before inserting the fork event, the database schema is inspected to determine whether the messages table has an agent_uuid column. This column was added by a later migration; older databases lack it.
If the column exists, a query counts messages where agent_uuid is null. If any such orphan messages exist, they are updated to reference the new root agent's UUID. This step preserves message history created before the agents table existed.
If the agent_uuid column does not exist, the adoption step is skipped.
behavior.fork-event-insertion:
A synthetic fork event message is inserted for the root agent with a role of "child". The event's data payload contains a JSON array of pinned paths pre-populated with the path to the system prompt file (system/prompt.md within the application data directory).
This event is not the result of an actual fork operation. It exists solely to allow the session replay mechanism to reconstruct the root agent's initial pinned document list during restoration, using the same replay path as forked agents.
The root agent has no parent and was never forked, but a fork event with role "child" is inserted to bootstrap its pinned document state into the replay system. The fork_message_id in this event is zero.
